title: What was Araels
wikitext:
[]: What was Arael's...             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Mon Oct 22, 2007 3:48 am purpose from the beginning? I mean, its weapon was obviously the A.T Field Ray Beam, and it was stationery and immobile. But, what was Arael's real purpose from the start? Even if the Angel did descend down into the atmosphere, how the hell could it break through the 22 layers of armour and also into the Geo-Front? Obviously, it would be of an enormous size in scale, so, how would it puncture through NERV if it doesn't seem to have any projectile or PHYSICALLY damaging weapons?

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:06 am plot device. Does this mean I lose my stripes as a fanwanker?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:07 am Not all of the Angels were after Adam or Lilith. Areal strikes me as being in this camp.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:09 am (hesitates)...OTHER THAN PLOT DEVICE! Like, being serious for a moment, if it actually contacted Lilith, HOW?

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:11 am mind beams kill Asuka Arael uses mind beams to synch with or contaminate 02 02 beats the hell out of everything and goes to terminal dogma. There. I've fanwanked something.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:14 am Maybe she just wasn't interested in Lilith. Seriously, they don't all have the same motives.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:16 am But, what was her motive?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:17 am Curiosity?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:17 am Ask Anno?

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:18 am One plausible motive would be to contact and better understand humans. Just look at the relevant dialog from the control room in that scene.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:22 am Let's roll. 8) :? :shock: Ach. So many questions in my head about Evangelion that's not in the Evangelion Fan-Geeks Project but even if I post all of them on this forum, EM would probably ban me from this site forever, including my IP Address. Curiosity? Well, perhaps that is one of her motives but, would it stay there forever? And even if NERV never did sortie EVA-02, what would it then do? Wait and float there until the 17th Angel appears? HA HA HA!

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:23 am Can't rule out the possibilty she was just looking for her family.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:26 am Family? Such as...? Looking for her husband, the 17th Angel? HA HA HA! You betcha, if Arael tried to "contact" (if you get my drift) the 17th Angel, it wouldn't be LCL that floods the Earth, but... :shock: :shock: :shock: :shock: :shock: JUST KIDDING!

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Oct 22, 2007 4:34 am Siblings, you know the other Angels. EVA-02 would seem like a relative to Areal, which was the intial target of the attack as it were.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Oct 22, 2007 7:05 am Of course, if Arael didn't have anything Impact-y directly in mind, it would arguably be plot device that brings her* straight to Tokyo-3 in the first place, as opposed to wandering around and probing for unborn siblings beneath the sea or something. * Or whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Mon Oct 22, 2007 8:24 am I think it was curiosity, in a similar way that Kaworu (i appologise if i've spelt that wrong) was curious about Shinji. I don't think it was looking for either Lilith or Adam. The Classified info states that not all the angels had the purpose of finding adam. Some had their own motives. I know it's second tier canon, but it's the best I can come up with.

[]:             Quote Post by Avatar of Dreams » Mon Oct 22, 2007 1:28 pm Indigo Arcangel wrote: I think it was curiosity, in a similar way that Kaworu (i appologise if i've spelt that wrong) was curious about Shinji. I don't think it was looking for either Lilith or Adam. The Classified info states that not all the angels had the purpose of finding adam. Some had their own motives. I know it's second tier canon, but it's the best I can come up with.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Mon Oct 22, 2007 1:46 pm Ornette wrote: Curiosity?

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Mon Oct 22, 2007 3:49 pm Perhaps Arael’s motives where similar or connected to Armisael’s. I got the impression that Armisael had something different in mind than the other angels. She states that she is lonely. Even though lacking the “Fruit of Knowledge” keeps her from fully understanding loneliness she still understands the pain of it on a semi-instinctual level. The most logical explanation for her loneliness is because she is essentially the last of her kind (the naturally occurring angels). An angel based instrumentality would no longer serve a purpose for her because all the other angels are dead and no more can be born because of the destruction of Adam’s Chamber of Gaf. Instead of going for Lilith she opts to utilize the Lilim and their Evas. She commences bio fusion with Unit-00 and contacts Lilith’s complete soul (Rei synched with Unit-00). Using the AT field she attempts to resurrect her dead siblings. She then makes contact with Unit-01 (Lilith’s Body). Armisael has essentially contacted Lilith’s body and soul (equivalent to a Seed of Life) by fusing with the Evas. After this Armisael attempts instrumentality to remake the world for her and her resurrected siblings. This could explain why GNR appeared just before self destruction. Another theory I had which seems less likely is that Armisael was attempting a skewed instrumentality that would combine her with the Lilim. This would abate her loneliness and somewhat corroborate her dialogue. Even though SEELE says that the angels don’t act in a unified way I find this hard to believe. SEELE might not believe it themselves. Most of the later angels seem to build on the experience of the earlier ones. An example would be how Zeruel extends a protective cuticle over it’s core. When Arael probes the human mind and the Eva it might be gathering information to be potentially used by Armisael. Armisael utilizes this information to effortlessly fuse on both biological and psychological levels with the Evas to complete her goal. If Arael was allowed to continue than perhaps it would have attempted to contact Unit-01 and Unit-00 to bring about instrumentality similarly to Armisael. Armisael learns from the experience of Arael by using invasive tactics and by being highly mobile and swift unlike Arael. Paragraphs, for the love of Yui, paragraphs!

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Oct 22, 2007 6:18 pm Reichu wrote: Of course, if Arael didn't have anything Impact-y directly in mind, it would arguably be plot device that brings her* straight to Tokyo-3 in the first place, as opposed to wandering around and probing for unborn siblings beneath the sea or something. * Or whatever.
